# E2E Test Flow: Fresh Install to Weekly Generation

**Scenario:** New user installs plugin, gets credits, goes through wizard, enables weekly, and receives auto-generated story

**Test Environment:** Fresh WordPress install, clean database
**Duration:** ~15 minutes (excluding actual cron wait)
**Tester:** QA
**Date:** ___________
**Result:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

---

## Step-by-Step Flow

### Step 1: Plugin Activation
**Action:** Activate AI Story Maker plugin from Plugins page

**Expected Outcomes:**
- [ ] Plugin activates without errors
- [ ] No fatal PHP errors in error log
- [ ] Database tables created (check wp_aistma_* tables exist)
- [ ] Navigation menu updated with "AI Stories" or similar
- [ ] 5 startup credits assigned to domain (via gateway)
- [ ] Admin user sees welcome message

**Actual Outcome:** _______________
**Pass/Fail:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

---

### Step 2: Dashboard Visit - Activation Wizard Shows
**Action:** Admin logs in → Navigate to Dashboard

**Expected Outcomes:**
- [ ] Activation wizard modal appears (centered, overlay)
- [ ] Modal shows "Welcome to AI Story Maker"
- [ ] Title and description visible
- [ ] 10 default prompts listed
- [ ] "Next" or "Continue" button visible
- [ ] "Dismiss" button in corner
- [ ] No console errors

**Actual Outcome:** _______________
**Pass/Fail:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

---

### Step 3: Prompt Selection
**Action:** View the prompt list, click a prompt (e.g., "Adventure Story")

**Expected Outcomes:**
- [ ] Clicked prompt highlights (selected state)
- [ ] Other prompts are not highlighted
- [ ] Prompt description is visible
- [ ] Can switch to different prompt
- [ ] Selection is maintained until "Generate" clicked

**Actual Outcome:** _______________
**Pass/Fail:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

**Selected Prompt:** _______________

---

### Step 4: Generate Story
**Action:** Click "Generate Story" button with prompt selected

**Expected Outcomes:**
- [ ] Loading spinner appears
- [ ] AJAX request completes within 5 seconds (adjust based on API speed)
- [ ] Story preview appears in modal
- [ ] Preview shows:
  - [ ] Title (generated by AI)
  - [ ] Excerpt (first 150-200 chars)
  - [ ] Featured image (if API returns one)
- [ ] Credit count displayed: 5 → 4 (still 5, deduction on save)
- [ ] No console errors
- [ ] Preview modal is readable

**Actual Outcome:** _______________
**Pass/Fail:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

**Generated Title:** _______________

---

### Step 5: Credit Display Accuracy
**Action:** Note the credits displayed before and after generation

**Expected Outcomes:**
- [ ] Before generation: "5 credits remaining"
- [ ] After generation (before save): Still "5 credits remaining" (no deduction yet)
- [ ] Credits value matches gateway

**Actual Outcome:** _______________
**Pass/Fail:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

---

### Step 6: Save Post
**Action:** In preview modal, click "Save Story" button

**Expected Outcomes:**
- [ ] Loading spinner appears
- [ ] Success message shown: "Story saved!"
- [ ] Modal closes automatically
- [ ] Credit count now shows: 4 (1 deducted for save)
- [ ] Post created in WordPress (check Posts list)
- [ ] Post appears with generated title
- [ ] Post status is Published (or Draft, as designed)
- [ ] Featured image is attached
- [ ] Excerpt matches preview

**Actual Outcome:** _______________
**Pass/Fail:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

**Post ID:** _______________ 
**Post URL:** _______________

---

### Step 7: Rating Modal Display
**Action:** Generate and save 3 more stories (4 total, then 5th will trigger rating)

**Expected Outcomes:**
- [ ] First story: Credits 4 → 3
- [ ] Second story: Credits 3 → 2
- [ ] Third story: Credits 2 → 1
- [ ] After 5th save (4th here): Credits 1 → 0
- [ ] Rating modal appears after 5th generation/save

**Actual Outcome:** _______________
**Pass/Fail:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

**Note:** Stop if running out of credits before rating modal shows. May need to add more credits for full test.

---

### Step 8: Rating Modal Interaction
**Action:** When rating modal appears, rate the plugin

**Expected Outcomes:**
- [ ] Modal shows: "Love this plugin? Please rate us on WordPress.org"
- [ ] Star rating system is visible (1-5 stars)
- [ ] Clicking stars works (highlight shows selected rating)
- [ ] Optional feedback text field
- [ ] "Submit" button
- [ ] "Don't ask again" checkbox available
- [ ] Closing modal doesn't prevent story creation (click X or outside modal)

**Actual Outcome:** _______________
**Pass/Fail:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

**Rating Submitted:** ☐ Yes (_____ stars) ☐ No (Dismissed)

---

### Step 9: Weekly Generation Toggle
**Action:** After saving a story, look for "Enable Weekly" option

**Expected Outcomes:**
- [ ] Toggle/checkbox appears (location: dashboard, or after save?)
- [ ] Label reads: "Enable weekly auto-generation"
- [ ] Checkbox is **checked by default**
- [ ] Description explains: "Story will be generated every 7 days with selected prompt"
- [ ] Can toggle on/off
- [ ] No console errors

**Actual Outcome:** _______________
**Pass/Fail:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

---

### Step 10: Weekly Confirmation Modal
**Action:** Check the "Enable Weekly" checkbox (if not already checked)

**Expected Outcomes:**
- [ ] Confirmation modal appears immediately
- [ ] Modal title: "Enable weekly auto-generation?"
- [ ] Dropdown lists available prompts (same 10 defaults)
- [ ] Default prompt is pre-selected
- [ ] "Confirm" and "Cancel" buttons visible
- [ ] Modal is accessible (not behind other elements)

**Actual Outcome:** _______________
**Pass/Fail:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

---

### Step 11: Select Weekly Prompt
**Action:** In confirmation modal, select a prompt from dropdown (different from last used, if possible)

**Expected Outcomes:**
- [ ] Dropdown opens showing all prompts
- [ ] Can select any prompt
- [ ] Selected prompt highlights
- [ ] Selection is retained in dropdown

**Actual Outcome:** _______________
**Pass/Fail:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

**Selected Weekly Prompt:** _______________

---

### Step 12: Confirm Weekly
**Action:** Click "Confirm" button in weekly modal

**Expected Outcomes:**
- [ ] Modal closes
- [ ] Weekly toggle remains checked
- [ ] Success message: "Weekly generation enabled" (or similar)
- [ ] Selected prompt is now saved for weekly generation
- [ ] User meta updated: `_aistma_weekly_enabled` = true, `_aistma_weekly_prompt` = selected prompt
- [ ] No errors in logs

**Actual Outcome:** _______________
**Pass/Fail:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

---

### Step 13: Trigger Weekly Cron
**Action:** Manually trigger cron: `wp cron event run aistma_weekly_generation --user=<user_email>`
(Or wait 7 days if testing in production)

**Expected Outcomes:**
- [ ] Cron runs without fatal errors
- [ ] New post is generated with selected weekly prompt
- [ ] Post title differs from previous manual generation (different prompt)
- [ ] Post is created as Draft or Published (as designed)
- [ ] 1 credit is deducted (if user still has credits)
- [ ] `_aistma_weekly_last_generated` timestamp is updated
- [ ] Log shows: "Weekly generation completed for user X" (if logging)

**Actual Outcome:** _______________
**Pass/Fail:** ☐ PASS ☐ FAIL

**New Post ID:** _______________
**New Post Title:** _______________

---

## Notes

- Document any errors found in browser console
- Take screenshots of any bugs
- Record timing for API calls (should be <5 seconds)
- Verify gateway logs show credit deductions
- Check WordPress admin → Orders page (if gateway UI visible) to confirm credits
